title: Markets News, June 18, 2026: Stocks Rise to Finish Winning Week; Intel Leads Chip, Memory Stocks Higher; SpaceX Continues To Fall
extracted_text: Dow rose 0.1% Thursday, closing the holiday-shortened week up 0.7%. S&P 500 advanced 1.1% Thursday, weekly gain 0.9%. Nasdaq Composite jumped 1.9% Thursday, ending week 2.4% higher. Intel shares surged 11% after Trump Truth Social post claiming Apple agreed to work with Intel on US chip design/fabrication. Micron +9%, Marvell +8%. SpaceX fell >8% to $175, first daily losses since IPO. Kroger -8% on margin pressure (same-store sales +1% vs 3.2% YoY, EPS $1.46, revenue $46.1B, gross margin 22.7%). Accenture -16% on revenue miss ($18.7B) and guidance cut, Morgan Stanley downgrade. Oil: WTI $76.75/bbl flat, Brent $79.50 flat. Iran peace deal signed, Strait of Hormuz reopening. 10-year yield 4.46%. Bitcoin $63,100. Gold -3% to $4,235/oz. DXY 100.80 (+0.7%). Fed Chair Warsh: unambiguous and unanimous commitment to 2% inflation. BofA: clearly hawkish, much higher risk Fed will hike this year. SpaceX index inclusion: CRSP Monday June 22, S&P Dow Jones before Monday open, Russell/MSCI June 29. Juneteenth Friday — markets closed.
summary: Markets News, June 18, 2026: Stocks Rise to Finish Winning Week; Intel Leads Chip, Memory Stocks Higher; SpaceX Continues To Fall

title: SpaceX shares fall as post-IPO frenzy loses steam
extracted_text: SpaceX stock (SPCX.O) fell more than 6% on Thursday to $178.50, after a nearly 5% drop in prior session. Remains >30% above $135 IPO price. Market value of $2.52 trillion would shrink by >$150 billion on the day. IPO last week vaulted valuation past $2 trillion. Analysts warned of early volatility due to small public float and high valuation. IPOX Schuster analyst Kat Liu: 'Given the magnitude of the IPO and the strong initial performance, some degree of profit-taking is not surprising.' Other space companies also declined: Rocket Lab -3%, Planet Labs -3%, AST SpaceMobile -7%, Intuitive Machines -3%. Retail investors bought $300+ million net over first three sessions; only $9.1M net purchases Thursday per Vanda Research. SpaceX announced Tuesday acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI coding agent) for $60B in stock. Bankers preparing $20B+ bond offering for AI expansion.
summary: SpaceX shares fall as post-IPO frenzy loses steam

title: Intel stock rises after Trump touts U.S.-built chip deal with Apple
extracted_text: Intel shares spiked ~10% on Thursday after Trump Truth Social post claiming Apple and Intel agreed to design and build chips in the US. Trump also said Nvidia had agreed to build 'first level Chips' with Intel, and Elon Musk will build TerraFab — 'the largest Chip Factory in the World' — designed with Intel's technology team. Intel rose 8.8% premarket. 12-month gain +464%. Market cap $608.7B. Apple edged up 0.3%. PHLX Semiconductor Index up 90% YTD. CEO Lip-Bu Tan revitalized investor confidence. Intel had largely missed AI wave. CNBC requested comment from Intel, Apple, White House — no responses at publication time.
summary: Intel stock rises after Trump touts U.S.-built chip deal with Apple

title: A Corporate Spending Pullback Just Triggered Accenture's Worst Day Ever
extracted_text: Accenture stock heading for largest single-day percentage decrease on record. Q3 EPS $3.80 beat, revenue $18.7B miss. Full-year revenue growth forecast narrowed to 3%-4% from 3%-5%. Announced three cybersecurity acquisitions (Dragos majority stake, runZero, NetRise). Morgan Stanley downgraded to equal-weight, target $177 (from $240), citing AI spending crowding out IT services budgets. Stock down >50% YTD.
summary: A Corporate Spending Pullback Just Triggered Accenture's Worst Day Ever

title: Short-End U.S. Treasury Yields, Dollar Rise After Fed Hints at Rate Rises
extracted_text: Short-end Treasury yields rising, long-end yields falling after Fed turned more hawkish Wednesday. 2-year yield rose 3bp to 4.188%, 10-year fell 0.6bp to 4.456% per Tradeweb. DXY dollar index rose to 11-week high of 100.631. Fed left rates on hold but statement had more decisive language on price stability. Exness' Eric Chia: 'gains in the dollar could be limited by improving geopolitical sentiment.'
summary: Short-End U.S. Treasury Yields, Dollar Rise After Fed Hints at Rate Rises

title: Unstoppable Stock Market Rally Outruns Fed Policy Anxiety
extracted_text: DJIA up 0.7% for week, S&P 500 gained 0.9%, Nasdaq Composite rose 2.4%. June holding up despite hawkish Fed meeting. Market rally outrunning Fed policy anxiety.
summary: Unstoppable Stock Market Rally Outruns Fed Policy Anxiety

title: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Jumps, Oil Is Now Close to Pre-Iran Levels
extracted_text: US stocks poised to close week on strong note. Nasdaq jumped. Oil prices now close to pre-Iran war levels after peace deal signed.
summary: Stock Market Today: Nasdaq Jumps, Oil Is Now Close to Pre-Iran Levels

title: Oil settles at three-month low after Trump says deal signed to end Iran war
extracted_text: US and Iran signed MOU on peace deal per Trump. Deal includes reopening of Strait of Hormuz. Talks on final agreement expected in next 60 days. This is the background to Thursday's oil price action and Strait of Hormuz reopening.
summary: Oil settles at three-month low after Trump says deal signed to end Iran war

title: Ships surge out of Hormuz as Iran peace deal takes hold
extracted_text: Chinese and European ships spearhead traffic surge through Strait of Hormuz on Thursday following US-Iran peace agreement. Commercial shipping traffic accelerating out of the key oil chokepoint.
summary: Ships surge out of Hormuz as Iran peace deal takes hold

title: US-Iran deal takeaways: reopening the strait of Hormuz, waived oil sanctions
extracted_text: US officials revealed preliminary MOU between Washington and Tehran to end war. Reopening of Strait of Hormuz, waived oil sanctions on Iran. Deal signed Thursday June 18.
summary: US-Iran deal takeaways: reopening the strait of Hormuz, waived oil sanctions

title: U.S. filings for unemployment benefits fall to 226,000 last week as layoffs remain historically low
extracted_text: Jobless claims for week ending June 13 dropped by 4,000 to 226,000. In line with 225,000 forecast by FactSet analysts. Layoffs remain in historically low range. 4-week moving average rose by 4,000 to 223,250.
